when_to_use: Use the Present Perfect for past actions with a connection to NOW: an experience with no specific time given, a recent action with present relevance, or a situation continuing from the past until now.
when_to_use_ar: استخدم المضارع التام للأفعال الماضية المرتبطة بالحاضر: خبرة بدون وقت محدد، فعل حديث له أهمية الآن، أو موقف مستمر من الماضي للحاضر.
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: Arabic has no direct grammatical equivalent to this exact 'past-but-connected-to-now' concept — it's usually expressed with context or time words rather than a distinct verb form. Learners often default to Past Simple for everything, missing the deliberate ambiguity of time that Present Perfect signals (the WHEN doesn't matter — the fact or its present relevance does).

examples.en: I have visited Paris three times.
examples.ar: زرت باريس ثلات مرات (خبرة، الوقت غير محدد)

examples.en: She has just finished her homework.
examples.ar: هي خلصت واجبها لسه (فعل حديث، أهمية للحاضر)

examples.en: We have lived here for ten years.
examples.ar: احنا عايشين هنا من عشر سنين (مستمر من الماضي للحاضر)
جربت السوشي قبل كده؟ (سؤال عن خبرة)
هو لسه ما اتصلش بيا (متوقع، لسه محصلش)

common_errors.wrong: I have visited Paris last year.
common_errors.explanation: Present Perfect can NEVER be used with a specific past time word (last year, yesterday, in 2020) — those require Past Simple instead.

common_errors.correct: I have lived here since 2015. / I've been living here since 2015.
common_errors.explanation: A situation continuing from the past to now with 'since' or 'for' requires Present Perfect (or Present Perfect Continuous), not Present Continuous.

common_errors.wrong: She has go to the market.
common_errors.correct: She has gone to the market.
common_errors.explanation: The verb after have/has must be the past participle (3rd form) — 'gone', not the base form 'go'.

practice_fill_blank.hint: Experience, no specific time — Present Perfect.
Continuing from past to now with 'since'.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: We ___ (visit) Rome last summer. [careful — has a specific time!]
practice_fill_blank.hint: Specific past time word 'last summer' → Past Simple, not Present Perfect.

quick_rule: Present Perfect = connects the past to NOW. Never combine it with a specific past time (yesterday, in 2020, last week) — those need Past Simple. Signal words: ever, never, already, yet, just, for, since.
quick_rule_ar: المضارع التام بيربط الماضي بالحاضر. متستخدمهوش مع وقت ماضي محدد.
mnemonic: PRESENT Perfect = the PAST still matters in the PRESENT.
connect_to_speaking: Practice with your buddy: share 3 life experiences using 'I have...' (with no specific date), then share 1 specific memory using Past Simple (with a clear date/time) — feel the grammatical difference in real conversation.
